basic_info.title: Mother / EarthBound Beginnings
basic_info.review: A charming but flawed RPG that pioneered the quirky EarthBound style with its modern American setting and unique humor, though plagued by pacing issues and challenging gameplay.

play_today.details: Best modern option with official English localization, save states, and online play. Includes the complete MOTHER Restored fan translation patch.

recommended_patches.name: MOTHER 1+2 Fan Translation
recommended_patches.description: Complete English translation by Demi and Haeleth that translates all text including menus and story

recommended_patches.name: MOTHER Restored Patch
recommended_patches.description: Restores graphics and applies the fan translation, uncensors content for modern audiences

optimal_settings: Enable CRT filter for authentic NES look

play_today.details: Excellent portable option with official English localization and save states. Considered one of the best NES ports.

recommended_patches.name: MOTHER Restored Patch
recommended_patches.description: Restores graphics and applies the fan translation for complete English experience

play_today.details: Authentic original hardware experience. Best played with a CRT TV or emulator with accurate rendering.

recommended_patches.name: MOTHER 1+2 Fan Translation
recommended_patches.description: Complete English translation patch for the original Famicom ROM

optimal_settings: Use accurate mode for best compatibility

play_today.details: Avoid this version - community consensus states it's inferior in every way compared to the original NES version.

description.synopsis: A young boy named Ninten and his friends use psychic powers to save the world from alien invaders in this quirky RPG set in a parody of modern America.
key_features: Modern American setting unlike typical fantasy RPGs
Quirky humor and pop culture references
Innovative save system with portable devices
First game in the Mother series
description.long_description: Mother, released as EarthBound Beginnings outside Japan, is a 1989 role-playing video game developed by Ape Inc. and published by Nintendo for the Famicom/NES. Created by Shigesato Itoi (who also created EarthBound), it pioneered the quirky, modern-setting RPG style that would define the Mother series. The story follows Ninten, a boy with psychic powers who must save his town from alien invaders. Unlike typical fantasy RPGs of its era, Mother parodies American culture and features a contemporary setting. Despite receiving mixed reviews (IGN 6.5/10) due to pacing issues and challenging gameplay, it established the foundation for EarthBound and Mother 3. The game was never officially released in North America until the Wii U Virtual Console in 2015. A fan translation by Demi and Haeleth made the original accessible to English speakers worldwide. The GBA compilation 'Mother 1+2' is widely considered inferior to the original NES version according to community consensus.
legacy_and_impact: Established Shigesato Itoi as a pioneering RPG creator
Pioneered modern-setting parody RPGs in Japan
Created the foundation for the EarthBound series
Influenced indie games like Undertale and Omori
Never officially released in North America until 2015
Inspired countless fan translations and ROM hacks
Set the tone for quirky, character-driven RPGs
